question.text: The category of "sub-broker" in the Indian securities market has been:

answer.text: Merged with the category of Portfolio Manager
Discontinued and migrated to Authorized Person (AP) and/or Trading Member
Renamed as "Associate Broker" with the same functions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI discontinued the sub-broker category. Existing sub-brokers were required to migrate either to the Authorized Person (AP) category or to become Trading Members. An AP is a person appointed by a stock broker to provide access to the trading platform and acts as an agent of the stock broker. **The Trap:** The sub-broker category was not retained or renamed — it was completely discontinued. The migration was specifically to AP and/or Trading Member, not to Portfolio Manager. **Cross-Exam Context:** This regulatory change is particularly relevant for NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) and Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** An Authorized Person acts as an agent of the stock broker, providing access to the trading platform. Unlike the old sub-broker, the AP framework has clearer accountability through the appointing stock broker.
